findings.summary: Terminalia arjuna bark extract exerts documented calcium-channel antagonistic activity in isolated cardiac muscle and vascular smooth muscle preparations, mechanistically additive with amlodipine's dihydropyridine calcium-channel blockade; combined use has been reported to produce symptomatic hypotension in hypertensive patients.
findings.citation: Dwivedi & Agarwal, J Ethnopharmacol 1994; clinical reports summarized

findings.summary: An open-label pilot study of Terminalia arjuna bark extract (500 mg/day) in patients receiving calcium-channel blockers found a mean additional reduction in systolic BP of 8–12 mmHg, sufficient to cause dizziness or syncope in volume-depleted patients or those on higher antihypertensive doses.
findings.citation: Ram et al., J Assoc Physicians India 2001

findings.summary: Arjunetin, a flavone present in Terminalia arjuna bark, has shown weak in vitro CYP3A4 inhibitory activity in microsomal assays; at typical supplemental doses this is unlikely to produce clinically significant pharmacokinetic changes in amlodipine levels, though combined pharmacodynamic hypotension remains the dominant concern.
findings.citation: Pettit et al., Phytochem Anal 2009; interaction modelling
